definition: Move in a circular direction wholly or partly around an axis or point
example: The lady's not for turning.
source: Conservative Party Conference Speech

definition: The aggregate of people living together in a more or less ordered community
example: There is no such thing as society. There are individual men and women and there are families.
source: Interview for Woman's Own magazine
speech: Interview (often quoted as a speech line)

definition: A single human being as distinct from a group
example: There is no such thing as society. There are individual men and women and there are families.
source: Interview for Woman's Own magazine
speech: Interview (often quoted as a speech line)

definition: Able to accept or tolerate delays, problems, or suffering without becoming annoyed or anxious
example: I am extraordinarily patient, provided I get my own way in the end.
source: Various interviews and speeches

definition: The state of being free within society from oppressive restrictions
example: There can be no liberty unless there is economic liberty.
source: Various economic addresses

definition: Relating to economics or the economy
example: There can be no liberty unless there is economic liberty.
source: Various economic addresses

definition: A political and economic theory of social organization which advocates that the means of production, distribution, and exchange should be owned or regulated by the community as a whole
example: To cure the British disease with socialism was like trying to cure leukaemia with leeches.
source: Speech to the Conservative Party Conference

definition: A system of ideas and ideals, especially one which forms the basis of economic or political theory and policy
example: We must have an ideology. The other side have an ideology, they have a philosophy, they believe in it.
source: Speech to the Conservative Philosophy Group

definition: The study of the fundamental nature of knowledge, reality, and existence
example: We must have an ideology. The other side have an ideology, they have a philosophy, they believe in it.
source: Speech to the Conservative Philosophy Group

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show conviction when facing the greatest challenges.
source: Various (often in interviews about her worldview)

definition: A strong, hard magnetic silvery-grey metal
example: What Britain needs is an iron lady.
source: Soviet media nickname adopted and embraced
speech: Speeches after being dubbed the 'Iron Lady'

definition: Firm determination to do something
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show resolve when facing the greatest challenges.
source: Various interviews and speeches

definition: The state of being prosperous
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show prosperity when facing the greatest challenges.
source: Speech to the Scottish Conservative Conference

definition: Obtain (money) in return for labor or services
example: Pennies do not come from heaven. They have to be earned here on earth.
source: Speech to the Scottish Conservative Conference

definition: The position or office of a trustee
example: In his address, the orator urged his listeners to show trusteeship when facing the greatest challenges.
source: Speech to the Church of Scotland General Assembly
speech: Address to the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland

definition: An abundance of valuable possessions or money
example: It is not the creation of wealth that is wrong, but the love of money for its own sake.
source: Speech to the Church of Scotland General Assembly
speech: Address to the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland

definition: The action or process of bringing something into existence
example: It is not the creation of wealth that is wrong, but the love of money for its own sake.
source: Speech to the Church of Scotland General Assembly
speech: Address to the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland

definition: Profoundly immoral and wicked
example: I am in politics because of the conflict between good and evil, and I believe that in the end good will triumph.
source: Various (often in interviews about her worldview)

definition: A great victory or achievement
example: I am in politics because of the conflict between good and evil, and I believe that in the end good will triumph.
source: Various (often in interviews about her worldview)

definition: An instance of being defeated
example: Defeat? I do not recognize the meaning of the word.
source: Various (frequently quoted during the Falklands conflict)
